bug_data.description:
We used to support this, but it was removed in bug 1810097 to make fence delivery simpler (by only supporting same-process)

I want to restore the (de)serialization via file descriptor allowing us to send them cross process. This will *not* be the default method of (de)serializing hardware buffers. Hardware buffer `TextureClient`s/`TextureHost`s will still only be supported over same-process bridges when `AndroidHardwareBufferManager` is initialized: we'll keep just using an ID in `SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer`  from which the host end can look up the buffer in `AndroidHardwareBufferManager`. Fences will continue to work in this case as the client and host ends will use the same instance of `AndroidHardwareBuffer`.

But in cases we don't need to keep passing fences around (e.g. we want to send a buffer to another process for a single use) this will make that possible.

To achieve this we'll need to ensure that we can use `AndroidHardwareBuffer` when `AndroidHardwareBufferManager` is *not* initialized. This means only attempting to register the buffer if the manager is initialized, and making `AndroidHardwareBuffer` use its own `Monitor` rather than `AndroidHardwareBufferManager::GetMonitor()`
bug_data.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com

I want to restore the (de)serialization via file descriptor allowing us to send them cross process. This will *not* be the default method of (de)serializing hardware buffers. comments.author: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.raw_text:
AndroidHardwareBufferManager is used to look up AndroidHardwareBuffers
via an ID. This allows, for example, the host side of a same-process
IPDL bridge to create an AndroidHardwareBufferTextureHost from a
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer. We currently initialize this
only in the GPU process, when the (off by default) pref
"webgl.out-of-process.enable-ahardwarebuffer" has been enabled.

This patch makes it possible to create and use instances of the
AndroidHardwareBuffer class when AndroidHardwareBufferManager is not
initialized. These buffers may *not* be (de)serialized via
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer, but may be used in other ways.

This involves only registering a created buffer with the manager if
the manager is initialized, and making AndroidHardwareBuffer use its
own Monitor rather than the manager's. We additionally add missing
thread safety annotations to the monitors, and mark the monitors as
mutable thereby allowing some member functions to be const.

Bug 2013484 - Allow using AndroidHarwareBuffer without AndroidHardwareBufferManager. r?#gfx-reviewers


AndroidHardwareBufferManager is used to look up AndroidHardwareBuffers
via an ID. This allows, for example, the host side of a same-process
IPDL bridge to create an AndroidHardwareBufferTextureHost from a
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer. We currently initialize this
only in the GPU process, when the (off by default) pref
"webgl.out-of-process.enable-ahardwarebuffer" has been enabled.

This patch makes it possible to create and use instances of the
AndroidHardwareBuffer class when AndroidHardwareBufferManager is not
initialized. These buffers may *not* be (de)serialized via
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer, but may be used in other ways.

This involves only registering a created buffer with the manager if
the manager is initialized, and making AndroidHardwareBuffer use its
own Monitor rather than the manager's. We additionally add missing
thread safety annotations to the monitors, and mark the monitors as
mutable thereby allowing some member functions to be const.
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com

comments.text:
Created attachment 9541216
Bug 2013484 - Allow (de)serializing AndroidHardwareBuffer to/from file descriptor. r?#gfx-reviewers


This used to be supported but was removed in bug 1810097 to ensure
fence delivery remained simple for out-of-process webgl, which was the
only use of AndroidHardwareBuffer.

This patch reinstates the ability to serialize/deserialize
AndroidHardwareBuffers to/from file descriptors, which can be sent
cross-process. However, it comes with the caveat that fences will not
be delivered between the two AndroidHardwareBuffer instances. This is
therefore opt-in rather than the default method of (de)serializing
hardware buffers.

Normal usage via TextureClient/TextureHost continues to use
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer for (de)serialization, which
can only be used over same-process bridges when
AndroidHardwareBufferManager is enabled (in which case fences are
delivered as expected).

Since code may attempt to lock and subsequently read or write from the
buffer, when deserializing we populate the size, stride, and format
from the description obtained by AHardwareBuffer_describe() to ensure
their values are trusted. If the format is not recognized then we fail
to create the buffer.
comments.creator: jnicol@mozilla.com
comments.author: jnicol@mozilla.com

be delivered between the two AndroidHardwareBuffer instances. This is
therefore opt-in rather than the default method of (de)serializing

Normal usage via TextureClient/TextureHost continues to use

can only be used over same-process bridges when
AndroidHardwareBufferManager is enabled (in which case fences are
delivered as expected).

Since code may attempt to lock and subsequently read or write from the
buffer, when deserializing we populate the size, stride, and format
from the description obtained by AHardwareBuffer_describe() to ensure
their values are trusted. If the format is not recognized then we fail
to create the buffer.

Bug 2013484 - Allow using AndroidHarwareBuffer without AndroidHardwareBufferManager. r?#gfx-reviewers


AndroidHardwareBufferManager is used to look up AndroidHardwareBuffers
via an ID. This allows, for example, the host side of a same-process
IPDL bridge to create an AndroidHardwareBufferTextureHost from a
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer. We currently initialize this
only in the GPU process, when the (off by default) pref
"webgl.out-of-process.enable-ahardwarebuffer" has been enabled.

This patch makes it possible to create and use instances of the
AndroidHardwareBuffer class when AndroidHardwareBufferManager is not
initialized. These buffers may *not* be (de)serialized via
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer, but may be used in other ways.

This involves only registering a created buffer with the manager if
the manager is initialized, and making AndroidHardwareBuffer use its
own Monitor rather than the manager's. We additionally add missing
thread safety annotations to the monitors, and mark the monitors as
mutable thereby allowing some member functions to be const.

comments.text:
Created attachment 9541216
Bug 2013484 - Allow (de)serializing AndroidHardwareBuffer to/from file descriptor. r?#gfx-reviewers


This used to be supported but was removed in bug 1810097 to ensure
fence delivery remained simple for out-of-process webgl, which was the
only use of AndroidHardwareBuffer.

This patch reinstates the ability to serialize/deserialize
AndroidHardwareBuffers to/from file descriptors, which can be sent
cross-process. However, it comes with the caveat that fences will not
be delivered between the two AndroidHardwareBuffer instances. This is
therefore opt-in rather than the default method of (de)serializing
hardware buffers.

Normal usage via TextureClient/TextureHost continues to use
SurfaceDescriptorAndroidHardwareBuffer for (de)serialization, which
can only be used over same-process bridges when
AndroidHardwareBufferManager is enabled (in which case fences are
delivered as expected).

Since code may attempt to lock and subsequently read or write from the
buffer, when deserializing we populate the size, stride, and format
from the description obtained by AHardwareBuffer_describe() to ensure
their values are trusted. If the format is not recognized then we fail
to create the buffer.

crashes.signature: EMPTY: no frame data available; EmptyMinidump

bug_analysis.root_cause: Support for (de)serializing AndroidHardwareBuffer across processes was removed in bug 1810097 to simplify fence delivery by only supporting same-process communication.
symptoms: Inability to send AndroidHardwareBuffer across process boundaries
Limited to same-process hardware buffer sharing
reproduction_steps: Attempt to send AndroidHardwareBuffer across processes
Observe failure due to lack of (de)serialization support
bug_analysis.technical_details: Restores (de)serialization of AndroidHardwareBuffer via file descriptors allowing cross-process transfer. Maintains same-process support through AndroidHardwareBufferManager with ID lookups. Modifies AndroidHardwareBuffer to operate without AndroidHardwareBufferManager initialization by using its own Monitor and conditional registration. Fences are only supported in same-process scenarios.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Enables sending Android hardware buffers across process boundaries for single-use cases, improving flexibility and inter-process communication involving graphics buffers.

common_patterns: Crashes related to memory allocation failures
OOM (Out Of Memory) conditions during garbage collection or resource handling
Crashes occurring in processes handling AndroidHardwareBuffer or related IPC serialization/deserialization
potential_root_causes: Insufficient memory management or leaks leading to OOM during GC
Improper handling or serialization of AndroidHardwareBuffer objects causing resource exhaustion
Inadequate eviction or allocation strategies in the memory nursery or buffer management
relevant_fix_clues: The mention of 'Failed to allocate new chunk during GC' points to garbage collector memory exhaustion
Bug 2013484 aims to allow (de)serializing AndroidHardwareBuffer across processes, implying that previous handling was incomplete or faulty
Fixes likely involve improving memory allocation strategies during GC and enhancing AndroidHardwareBuffer serialization to avoid leaks or overuse
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes occur primarily due to memory exhaustion during garbage collection or resource management involving AndroidHardwareBuffer serialization. The root cause appears to be insufficient or faulty memory handling in processes dealing with IPC of hardware buffers, leading to OOM crashes. Addressing these issues likely requires improving memory allocation and buffer serialization mechanisms to prevent resource leaks and reduce pressure on the garbage collector.

similar_bugs_data.summary: [meta] OOM crash evicting the nursery: "Failed to allocate new chunk during GC"

common_patterns: Crashes related to memory allocation failures

Crashes occurring in processes handling AndroidHardwareBuffer or related IPC serialization/deserialization
potential_root_causes: Insufficient memory management or leaks leading to OOM during GC

Inadequate eviction or allocation strategies in the memory nursery or buffer management
relevant_fix_clues: The mention of 'Failed to allocate new chunk during GC' points to garbage collector memory exhaustion
Bug 2013484 aims to allow (de)serializing AndroidHardwareBuffer across processes, implying that previous handling was incomplete or faulty
Fixes likely involve improving memory allocation strategies during GC and enhancing AndroidHardwareBuffer serialization to avoid leaks or overuse
similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes occur primarily due to memory exhaustion during garbage collection or resource management involving AndroidHardwareBuffer serialization. The root cause appears to be insufficient or faulty memory handling in processes dealing with IPC of hardware buffers, leading to OOM crashes. Addressing these issues likely requires improving memory allocation and buffer serialization mechanisms to prevent resource leaks and reduce pressure on the garbage collector.

refined_analysis.root_cause: Cross-process (de)serialization of AndroidHardwareBuffer was removed in bug 1810097, preventing buffer sharing across process boundaries.
symptoms: AndroidHardwareBuffer cannot be transferred between processes
Cross-process hardware buffer sharing fails due to missing IPC (de)serialization
reproduction_steps: Attempt to send AndroidHardwareBuffer via IPC between processes
Observe failure due to lack of cross-process (de)serialization support
refined_analysis.technical_details: Re-enable cross-process (de)serialization of AndroidHardwareBuffer by passing native handles over Unix domain sockets in IPC channels. Retain same-process sharing via AndroidHardwareBufferManager using buffer ID lookup. Refactor AndroidHardwareBuffer (gfx/layers/AndroidHardwareBuffer.cpp/h) to support construction without mandatory AndroidHardwareBufferManager registration by adding an internal Monitor for thread safety and conditional registration. Maintain fence synchronization limited to same-process contexts to avoid complexity. Update IPC serialization and deserialization code in ipc/ to support file descriptor passing of AndroidHardwareBuffer handles.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Restores cross-process sharing of Android hardware buffers, improving graphics IPC flexibility and performance.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: Implement (de)serialization of AndroidHardwareBuffer via file descriptor passing over Unix sockets in IPC actors (gfx/layers/AndroidHardwareBuffer.cpp/h and ipc/). Modify AndroidHardwareBuffer to allow construction without registration to AndroidHardwareBufferManager by adding an internal Monitor and conditional registration logic. Maintain fence synchronization only for same-process sharing. Update IPC actors to pass native handles using Unix domain sockets.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: Re-enable cross-process (de)serialization of AndroidHardwareBuffer removed in bug 1810097 by implementing native handle transfer over Unix sockets in IPC actors. Modify AndroidHardwareBuffer (gfx/layers/AndroidHardwareBuffer.cpp/h) to support instantiation without mandatory AndroidHardwareBufferManager registration by adding an internal Monitor and conditional registration. Limit fence synchronization to same-process contexts. Update IPC serialization code in ipc/ to support file descriptor passing for AndroidHardwareBuffer transfer. This restores cross-process graphics buffer sharing and IPC functionality.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The analysis covers root cause, symptoms, and a detailed fix approach but is verbose and partially redundant. It only lists key files and directories without pinpointing exact functions or code paths, reducing AI fix precision. The reproduction steps are minimal but adequate. The technical details and proposed fix could be more concise and focused on actionable implementation steps. The optimized_context is lengthy and could be shorter with clearer references to specific serialization/deserialization functions and IPC actor locations. Overall, the analysis partially meets artifact hints and self-contained criteria but needs sharpening for conciseness and stronger actionable guidance.

filtered_context.concise_summary: Re-enable cross-process (de)serialization of AndroidHardwareBuffer removed in bug 1810097 by implementing native handle transfer via file descriptors over Unix domain sockets in IPC actors. Modify AndroidHardwareBuffer (gfx/layers/AndroidHardwareBuffer.cpp/h) to allow construction without mandatory AndroidHardwareBufferManager registration by adding an internal Monitor and conditional registration. Limit fence synchronization to same-process contexts. Update IPC serialization/deserialization code in ipc/ to support passing AndroidHardwareBuffer handles as file descriptors, restoring cross-process graphics buffer sharing.
core_symptoms: AndroidHardwareBuffer cannot be transferred between processes
Cross-process hardware buffer sharing fails due to missing IPC (de)serialization
technical_constraints: Cross-process (de)serialization must use file descriptor passing over Unix domain sockets
AndroidHardwareBuffer must support instantiation without AndroidHardwareBufferManager registration
Fence synchronization limited to same-process sharing to avoid complexity
